description_long: This wooden temple features a complex, multi-tiered structure with three prominent onion-shaped domes, each topped with a spire. The central tower is the tallest, with a square base and a steeply pitched roof, flanked by two smaller towers of similar design. The exterior is clad in wooden shingles, giving the building a rustic, textured appearance. Windows are small and rectangular, set into the walls beneath the eaves. The rooflines cascade down in a series of steps, creating a layered silhouette typical of Eastern European wooden ecclesiastical architecture. The overall form is symmetrical along its main axis, with the main entrance likely located at the base of the central tower.
description: A multi-tiered wooden temple with three onion domes, steep shingled roofs, and a tall central tower, showcasing intricate Eastern European wooden architecture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the three onion domes, cascading shingled roofs, and the tall central tower with small windows.
description_view_1: Frontal view emphasizing the central tower's height, the main entrance area, and the symmetrical stepped roof structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the layered rooflines, wooden shingle texture, and the alignment of the domes.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the progression of roof tiers and the alignment of the domes along the building's axis.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist, with subtle wood shingle patterns and simple spire finials
attribute_roof_type: tiered onion dome with steep shingled gable roofs
attribute_entrance_design: rectangular doorway at the base of the central tower, framed by simple wooden trim and a small overhanging roof
